title: Quiz: Causes of Barren Plateaus
description: Test your understanding of what causes barren plateaus in variational quantum circuits

questions.question: What is an expressibility-induced barren plateau?

options.text: A barren plateau caused by too few parameters
A barren plateau caused by circuits that uniformly sample the state space
A barren plateau caused by measurement errors
A barren plateau caused by classical preprocessing

questions.explanation: Expressibility-induced barren plateaus occur when circuits form approximate 2-designs, uniformly sampling the state space and causing gradients to concentrate around zero.

questions.question: How does entanglement contribute to barren plateaus?

options.text: By reducing the number of parameters
By making local observables see nearly thermal (maximally mixed) states
By increasing circuit execution time
By causing decoherence

questions.explanation: High entanglement causes local subsystems to appear nearly maximally mixed, making local measurements insensitive to parameter changes.

questions.question: What type of noise causes noise-induced barren plateaus?

options.text: Only coherent errors
Only measurement errors
Depolarizing and other incoherent noise channels
Classical bit-flip errors only

questions.explanation: Incoherent noise channels like depolarizing noise push quantum states toward the maximally mixed state, causing additional gradient suppression beyond the unitary case.

questions.question: Local cost functions (measuring few qubits) have exponentially suppressed gradients like global cost functions.

options.text: True
False

questions.explanation: Local cost functions have polynomially decaying gradients (mild suppression), while global cost functions have exponentially decaying gradients (severe suppression).

questions.question: How does hardware connectivity affect barren plateaus?

options.text: Limited connectivity always makes barren plateaus worse
All-to-all connectivity helps avoid barren plateaus
Limited connectivity can help avoid barren plateaus by reducing entanglement
Connectivity has no effect on barren plateaus

questions.explanation: Limited hardware connectivity (like linear nearest-neighbor) reduces the circuit's ability to generate high entanglement, which can help avoid barren plateaus.

questions.question: What is a 2-design in the context of quantum circuits?

options.text: A circuit with exactly 2 layers
An ensemble whose 2nd moments match Haar-random unitaries
A circuit using only 2-qubit gates
A design pattern for 2-qubit systems

questions.explanation: A 2-design is an ensemble of unitaries whose statistical moments up to order 2 match those of Haar-random unitaries, indicating maximum expressibility.

questions.question: If a circuit is too shallow to form a 2-design, what can still cause barren plateaus?

options.text: Nothing - shallow circuits never have barren plateaus
Global cost functions and high entanglement can still cause barren plateaus
Only classical optimization can cause barren plateaus in shallow circuits
Shallow circuits always have larger gradients

questions.explanation: Even shallow circuits can exhibit barren plateaus if they use global cost functions or generate significant entanglement, though typically less severe than deep circuits.

questions.question: Which factors increase the severity of barren plateaus? (Select all that apply)

options.text: Increasing circuit depth
Using local cost functions
Adding more entangling gates
Increasing noise rates

questions.explanation: Deeper circuits (a), more entanglement (c), and higher noise (d) all worsen barren plateaus. Local cost functions (b) actually help mitigate barren plateaus.
